Title: The miR156/SPL12 module orchestrates fruit colour change through directly regulating ethylene production pathway in blueberry

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit peel; tissue culture seedling basal part
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ethylene biosynthesis linked to fruit colour change
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ACC synthesis branch of ethylene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ACS enzyme family, PLP-dependent aminotransferase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid (ACC), the immediate ethylene precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor that promotes ethylene production and thereby fruit colour change
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; ACS is described in Discussion as a rate-limiting enzyme for ethylene production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher VcACS1 expression is associated with increased ACC/ethylene production, enhanced anthocyanin accumulation, and reduced chlorophyll during fruit colour change; VcSPL12 represses it
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high at early fruit developmental stages, then decreases from light green stage to ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcriptionally upregulated by VcMIR156a overexpression; downregulated in VcSPL12-overexpressing lines and transient VcSPL12-overexpressing fruits; elevated in VcSPL12-silenced fruits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native VcACS1 promoter; promoter fragments used in Y1H and dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon; ACC; 1-MCP; CoCl2; fruit developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Y1H; promoter deletion analysis; dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; transient VcSPL12 overexpression; VIGS silencing; ACC quantification; pigment profiling under ethylene substitute/inhibitor treatments
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VcACS1 is an ethylene biosynthetic gene negatively regulated by VcSPL12. 2. VcSPL12 directly binds the VcACS1 promoter and represses its transcriptional activity. 3. Reduced VcACS1 expression is linked to lower ACC/ethylene production, less anthocyanin accumulation, and more chlorophyll retention during blueberry fruit colour change.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested ACS family members, VcACS1 was one of the important fruit-expressed members; multiple SPL-binding sites in promoter were required collectively for full VcSPL12 binding.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or weakening VcACS1 promoter activity, or increasing VcSPL12 action on VcACS1, could delay de-greening and anthocyanin accumulation; conversely releasing repression may accelerate coloration.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit peel; tissue culture seedling basal part
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ethylene biosynthesis linked to fruit colour change
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ACO enzyme family, Fe(II)/2-oxoglutarate-dependent oxygenase-like ethylene-forming enzyme family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes ACC to ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms ethylene, which promotes anthocyanin accumulation and chlorophyll loss during fruit colour change
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; ACO is described in Discussion as a rate-limiting enzyme for ethylene production; VcACO6 is proposed as a major family member for ethylene burst in fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher VcACO6 expression is linked to increased ethylene formation and fruit colour change; VcSPL12 directly represses it, delaying anthocyanin accumulation and maintaining chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: very low at early fruit stages and strongly induced from light green stage to pink/blue ripening stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated by VcMIR156a overexpression; repressed in transient VcSPL12-overexpressing fruits and induced in VcSPL12-silenced fruits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native VcACO6 promoter; promoter fragments used in Y1H and dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon; ACC; 1-MCP; CoCl2; fruit developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y1H; promoter motif deletion; dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; transient VcSPL12 overexpression; VIGS silencing; ACC quantification; developmental expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VcACO6 is a direct VcSPL12 target in the ethylene pathway. 2. VcSPL12 binds a GTAC-containing promoter site and represses VcACO6 transcription. 3. VcACO6 shows ripening-associated induction and is proposed to be a major ACO member contributing to ethylene burst and fruit coloration in blueberry.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights VcACO6 as the highest-abundance tested ACO transcript in fruit and a likely major isoform for ripening-associated ethylene burst.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: VcACO6 is a strong target for tuning ripening pace and berry coloration; promoter editing of SPL-binding motifs or modulation through VcMIR156a/VcSPL12 could adjust ethylene burst timing.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: green coloration and photosynthetic pigment maintenance
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DVR enzyme family, reductase involved in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces divinyl chlorophyllide/chlorophyll intermediates to monovinyl forms in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: divinyl chlorophyllide a or related divinyl chlorophyll intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: monovinyl chlorophyllide a or related monovinyl chlorophyll intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: VcDVR expression promotes chlorophyll accumulation; VcSPL12 directly activates VcDVR, contributing to green colour retention and opposing anthocyanin-dominant coloration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: downregulated in VcMIR156a-overexpressing seedlings; upregulated in VcSPL12-overexpressing lines and transiently overexpressing fruits; reduced in VcSPL12-silenced fruits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native VcDVR promoter fragment pVcDVR-1 used in Y1H and dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; transient VcSPL12 overexpression; VIGS silencing; chlorophyll measurement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VcDVR is a chlorophyll biosynthetic gene positively associated with chlorophyll accumulation in blueberry. 2. VcSPL12 directly binds the VcDVR promoter and enhances its transcriptional activity. 3. Activation of VcDVR by VcSPL12 supports chlorophyll retention during fruit growth and delays visible colour transition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is directly targeted and functionally validated in the Results, although the paper's main nutrient-metabolism focus is fruit colour transition through ethylene and anthocyanin/chlorophyll balance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: VcDVR can be used together with VcSPL12 modulation to maintain green fruit colour longer or alter de-greening kinetics.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional cleavage/repression of target SPL transcript
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ethylene biosynthesis upstream control; anthocyanin accumulation promotion; chlorophyll accumulation suppression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses VcSPL12, thereby indirectly increasing ethylene biosynthetic genes including VcACS1 and VcACO6 and promoting anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VcSPL12 transcript as validated miR156 cleavage target
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: previous study suggested VcMIR156a is a major source of mature miR156 at onset of fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overexpression lines show elevated anthocyanins, reduced chlorophylls, increased ethylene biosynthetic gene expression and higher methionine/SAM/ACC levels
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon; ACC; 1-MCP; CoCl2; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anthocyanin accumulation, reduces chlorophyll accumulation, and elevates ethylene precursor levels by repressing VcSPL12
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: developmental upstream regulator that initiates fruit colour change by relieving VcSPL12 repression on ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression in blueberry; transcriptomics; metabolomics; qRT-PCR; degradome/RACE from previous work for target validation; pharmacological ethylene manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VcMIR156a overexpression increases anthocyanins and decreases chlorophylls in blueberry. 2. It elevates ethylene precursor accumulation and induces ethylene biosynthetic genes. 3. Its colour-change function operates largely through repression of VcSPL12 and consequent activation of the ethylene production pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a miRNA rather than TF, it is a core regulator in the paper and functions upstream of VcSPL12.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: determines fruit colour transition during growth and ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of ethylene biosynthetic genes and transcriptional activation of a chlorophyll biosynthetic gene
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ethylene biosynthesis; chlorophyll biosynthesis; indirect control of anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses VcACS1 and VcACO6 promoter activity; activates VcDVR promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: repressed by VcMIR156a-derived miR156; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VcACS1 promoter; VcACO6 promoter; VcDVR promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high at early fruit developmental stages and sharply reduced from the light green stage onward
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stable overexpression increases chlorophyll and decreases anthocyanins in seedlings; transient overexpression in fruit lowers ACC and delays coloration; silencing causes red coloration and higher ACC
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon; ACC; 1-MCP; CoCl2; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suppresses anthocyanin accumulation and ethylene production while promoting chlorophyll accumulation, thereby delaying or opposing fruit colour change
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major switch connecting miR156 signalling to ethylene production and pigment metabolism during blueberry fruit colour change
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression in blueberry; transient overexpression in fruit; VIGS silencing; qRT-PCR; Y1H; promoter motif deletion; dual-luciferase assay; ACC HPLC-MS/MS; pigment profiling; pharmacological ethylene treatments
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR-centered validation; linked to RNA-seq/metabolomics results from VcMIR156a lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S/pBI121 overexpression construct; pTRV VIGS constructs in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VcSPL12 acts downstream of VcMIR156a and negatively regulates fruit colour change. 2. It directly represses ethylene biosynthetic genes VcACS1 and VcACO6, lowering ACC/ethylene production. 3. It also directly activates VcDVR, promoting chlorophyll accumulation and green colour maintenance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous work cited in the paper reported physical interaction with VcMYBPA1 but not VcMYBA; in this paper, direct validated metabolic targets are VcACS1, VcACO6, and VcDVR.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising regulatory node for tuning ripening time, de-greening, and anthocyanin buildup in blueberry via overexpression, editing of miR156 target site, or promoter engineering.
